title: Cell-culture protocol set (passaging, cryopreservation, mycoplasma testing)
context: Cell-line culture methods (passaging, cryopreservation, mycoplasma testing) are foundational to virtually all cancer cell-biology research and are a primary source of reproducibility failures due to cross-contamination, incorrect passaging intervals, and mycoplasma contamination. These methods involve biohazardous material (BSL-1/2 cell lines), cryogenic hazards (liquid nitrogen), and DMSO handling, requiring rigorous safety documentation. Human-derived cell lines may require IRB/ethics flags. This is the largest task in the M2 seed library.
objective: Author, validate, lint, and ship a set of cell-culture protocols (passaging, cryopreservation, mycoplasma testing) through the full pipeline, each schema-valid, linter-green, with 100% provenance, complete safety sections (including BSL designation), human-derived material ethics flags where applicable, and credentialed safety sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: At least three protocols authored: cell passaging, cryopreservation (with liquid-nitrogen handling), and mycoplasma testing; each schema-valid and linter-green.
Human-derived cell-line steps flagged with ethics.humanDerivedMaterial:true and IRB/consent pointer where applicable; no donor data included.
Biohazard handling, BSL level, and waste disposal cited to BMBL/OSHA per step; credentialed safety sign-off recorded.
100% of steps and safety claims carry resolvable, license-verified provenance references.
License+Ethics gate PASS per protocol; dual-use screen clear; all mandatory disclaimers present.
semver v1.0.0 + CHANGELOG per protocol; rendered Markdown/HTML produced; CI green; commits DCO signed-off.
resources: NIH/CDC BMBL 6th ed. (BSL-1/2 cell culture, cryogenic hazards)
ATCC cell line handling guidelines (openly available)
protocols.io (per-protocol CC-BY source candidates — license must be verified per item)
output: Committed, fully-reviewed cell-culture protocol records (passaging, cryopreservation, mycoplasma testing) with rendered outputs, license evidence, ethics flags, and all review sign-offs; CI green; commits DCO signed-off.
